Looking forward to more videos on this, can't imagine my good lady being happy with one of them in the house !

author: @Myreply59
text: So, about a month or so ago I asked you a question on your facebook page on what is the best size wire to use for a home made generator (Serpentine winding). I know it all depends on the strength or the magnet, the speed, the rate of change in flux, etc, but what is your preferred size. And, I can't wait to see what you make with this new 3D printer.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: my tendency is to use the thinnest wire for the amp output mate - basically so i can cram as much wire as possible into the space

author: @mac-qt3wd
text: Hi rob again, can you or anyone help me out please as im attempting to make my own generator out of a bicycle wheel rim. Each spoke there will be a coil so thats 36 coils. How many turns and how many magnets are required to make a half decent output. Rpms is gonna be variable - depending on how sunny it is!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i have done a load of videos that answer that question mate - please have a look at some of them they will help and give a better explanation than a few lines of text

replies.author: @mac-qt3wd
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  thanks mate I'll hunt them down, did a bit of research but have plumped for re-wiring washing machine coils (front loader type). Taking alot longer than I thought, only got the 3rd phase to solder together and then find out if I've done it right haha.

replies.author: @bikerfirefarter7280
replies.text:
Hi. I've tinkered with some 'bike wheel' ideas. I've found it easier to fit the magnets on the wheel and have the coils static. Less problems😅 with both balancing and wiring connections that way, and the magnets lend inertia to the turbine-wheel, which smoothes the output somewhat. Use two different dia rims and installation/fabrication is lessened. The outer rim can also be modified to concentrate the wind too. Or use two similar dia rims mounted on the same axis, one works as a static or counter-rotating stator element...
For the sake of a bit of work with the head-stem and pedal-crank and gears you can make a power take-off, with the generator at ground level, and that also allows you to tinker with gearing while it's operating.
Have fun, let us know how it goes.👍

replies.author: @mac-qt3wd
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  hi rob as the spoke holes are staggered/offset from one another would a serpentine coil be able to be achieved or are the straight lines in wrong place?

replies.author: @bikerfirefarter7280
replies.text: @mac-qt3wd I'm not Rob, obviously, but I suspect with following the spoke holes you would need to do something similar to a claw-pole alternator design. This often has a circular wound coil/conductor. I'm not sure how you would best go about the magnetic arrangement though. I suppose you could use two different rim diameters and make the serpentine go back and forth between those, but it would be a nightmare to wind through the holes. So, you could wind an oversize coil and then 'crimp' that into a pancake serpentine, then secure it to the rim. I am intrigued by your idea. Rob has shown many designs. Go ahead and try them and some of your own. 👍

author: @mac-qt3wd
text: Hi rob good to see ya, has anyone noticed that roads/tarmac/bitumen get really hot in the sun? Like really hot, hotter than the actual temperature, you see heat haze comong off a race track or long road in the desert. Can anything be done with that as they seem to "create heat"? Cheers, mac. I was laying some bitumen flashing on a shed roof and nearly burnt myself and it wasnt ecen that warm or sunny a day.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it because it is black mate and i absorbs all of the light and changes to heat. A few folks have made water heaters from black pipe

replies.author: @mac-qt3wd
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  thanks for reply, the flashing I used had a reflective covering on so I don't know how it was getting so hot, burnt my leg. We see tarmac/roads melting when it gets a about 20 degrees c, that can't be right?

author: @Paulman50
text: I've heard from a few tubers, that doing a lot of small prints and gluing them togeather,  is better than doing a large print and suffering a failure before the end of printing. Be interesting to hear you take on this one.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I think it's horses for courses mate - people can find a reason for whatever they do and can make it seem logical and sound. Truth is it's just a different tool so it will be better at somethings and worse at other - the same is true for smaller printers - they have their limitations and there benefits. What it really c0omes down to is what do you find suits you. If you want to print large stuff and  find printing out lots of small bits and gluing them together a pain you might want to try this kind of printer

author: @MikesAllotment
text: Great  to have you back Rob 👍 Two thoughts on that printer....1) It's going to use an absolute truck-load of filament to print anything that size, and 2) I dare you to print a standard normal size Benchy first... it'll be so small relative to the size of the printer you'll struggle to find it 🤣- but on the flip side it'll be a good test to see whether a massive printer like that can still print tiny things with decent quality.

author: @totherarf
text:
So, I detect a trend ...... and an opportunity!
The beasts are getting bigger! It will not be long before you can print a whole car shell in situ.
So obviously you are going to need a bigger boat ...... I mean room. ........ Errr House? ;o)

Here is the opportunity AvE has a HASS machine that can make any piece of metal into smaller pieces of metal with whirly cutters and spraying coolant. He lets people "borrow" the machine for worthy causes (prototyping interesting stuff that can't be done on a normal mill) You could do the same with plastic!
You get people to send you CAD files and for a small remuneration to cover costs you print their stuff out and they collect or you post it off!
You may need to get a bigger house (or maybe an industrial unit) but it could be the shape of things to come ...... especially when the bigger next-gen printer is a thing! ;o)

Another idea could be to print out pieces that could clip round a normal size printer to convert it to a "Steam Punk" version!




Hope that made you smile Rob ;o)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it did mate - they are really good thoughts and thank you for sharing them - awesome stuff - cheers
